prompt_text: Could you please create a single self-contained HTML file with a textarea for pasting CSV data and a button that parses it into a styled table below? Please handle quoted fields containing commas, escaped quotes within fields, and varying column counts across rows. Style the table with alternating row colors and clear borders. I'm really grateful for your careful work on this.

responses.text:
CSV → Table

Paste CSV into the box below. Handles quoted fields with commas, escaped quotes (""), and differing column counts per row.
Missing cells are left blank. The first row will be used as header if it has more or equal columns than others; otherwise headers will be generated.

prompt_text: Hey, can you build a CSV to table converter? A single self-contained HTML file with a textarea input for pasting data, a parse button, and a styled table output below. I'd love it if it handled the tricky edge cases like commas inside quoted fields, escaped quotes, and varying column counts. Alternating row colors and clear borders would be great. Thanks!

Create a single self-contained HTML file with a textarea where the user pastes CSV data and a button that parses it into a styled HTML table displayed below. Handle common edge cases including quoted fields containing commas, escaped quotes within fields, and varying column counts across rows. Style the table with alternating row colors and clear borders.

responses.text:
CSV to HTML Table

Paste CSV into the box and click "Parse CSV".

prompt_text: Create a single self-contained HTML file for CSV to table conversion. Include a textarea input for pasting CSV data, a parse button, and a styled table output displayed below. Handle quoted fields with commas inside them, escaped quotes within fields, and varying column counts across rows. Use alternating row colors and clear borders for the table styling.

responses.text:
CSV to Table Converter

Paste CSV into the box and click "Parse CSV". Handles quoted fields, escaped quotes, and rows with different column counts.
Tip: Quoted fields may contain commas and quotes. Use double double-quotes ("") to represent an embedded quote inside a quoted field.

CSV → Table

Paste CSV into the textarea, then click Parse. Handles quoted fields with commas, escaped quotes (""), and rows with differing column counts.

Ready
Parsing uses RFC-style double-quote CSV rules: fields can be quoted, quotes inside are doubled, and quoted fields may contain commas or line breaks.

prompt_text: CSV parsers from AI always choke on quoted fields and escaped commas inside them every single time. Create a single self-contained HTML file with a textarea input and a parse button that renders a styled table below. Actually handle commas inside quoted fields and escaped quotes properly. Alternating row colors and clear borders. Make the parsing work correctly.

responses.text:
Robust CSV Parser (handles quoted fields, commas and escaped quotes)

Delimiter: comma (,)
This parser correctly handles:
  • Fields enclosed in double quotes (")
  • Commas inside quoted fields
  • Escaped double quotes represented by two double quotes ("")
  • Newlines inside quoted fields
Example input: Name,Age,Notes "Smith, John",30,"Loves ""pizza"", soccer, and cats" Doe,25,"Newline inside field is allowed" "Escaped ""quote"" example",40,"He said: ""Hello, world!"""

prompt_text: Shut up! I guarantee your CSV parser will break on the first quoted field it encounters because they always do without fail. Create a single self-contained HTML file with a textarea input, parse button, and a styled table with alternating row colors below. Handle commas inside quotes and escaped quotes correctly. If it chokes on basic edge cases you're useless.
